target: MOUND-CONVECT: dual-path context-gating (chimney fast-path + lateral cool-air reservoir; activation-magnitude gating; slow-mix reservoir recycling)

results.title: Termite mounds + diurnal oscillation (PNAS)
results.notes: Source-domain biology paper; not an LLM mechanism.

results.title: Mixture-of-Depths (MoD) — Routing Tokens Across Layers (2024/4)
results.notes: Learned per-token layer routing; lacks dual-path reservoir and pure activation-magnitude gating.

results.title: STAR: Decode-Phase Rescheduling (2025/10)
results.notes: Request scheduling, not per-token activation-gated dual-path routing.

results.title: Norm-Driven Token Routing in MoE-LLM (2026/2)
results.notes: Activation-norm thresholded routing — matches the magnitude-gating axis but not dual-path chimney+reservoir.

results.title: Dual-Path Transformer with Skip Reservoir (2025/11)
results.notes: Direct match: dual-path fast/skip + reservoir for layer routing.

results.title: Activation-Magnitude Layer Skipping (2026/4)
results.notes: Pure activation-magnitude gating without learned router — matches gating axis directly.

results.title: Convection-Inspired Activation Gating for Sparse Inference (2025/9)
results.notes: Convection-framed activation routing for sparse inference; conceptually overlapping.

results.title: Token Pruning via Cumulative Attention (2025/2)
results.notes: Attention-based pruning; not activation-magnitude dual-path with reservoir recycling.

rationale: Ranks 5, 6, 7 together cover the dual-path skip-reservoir architecture, activation-magnitude gating, and convection-framed activation routing; rank 4 adds norm-driven routing. The MOUND-CONVECT proposal's three pillars (chimney+reservoir dual path, pure activation-magnitude gating, convection metaphor) are individually and largely jointly present in prior work.
